title: dianthus caryophyllus var. collivagus
intro: Dianthus caryophyllus var. collivagus, also known as the fragrant carnation, is a variety of the Dianthus caryophyllus plant, a flowering perennial native to the Mediterranean region. The plant's flowers and leaves have been used in herbal medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils. The health benefits of Dianthus caryophyllus var. collivagus include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin conditions and respiratory issues. Dianthus caryophyllus var. collivagus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
